meta.name: Type A — The Wraparound Residence
meta.tagline: Corner residences with panoramic golf-course frontage.
meta.description: Corner-positioned residences with wraparound terraces and dual aspects across the Golf Drive and the landscaped Sanctuary. Best for clients prioritising light, view depth and entertainment-led plans.
meta.useWhen: Pitch when a client prioritises corner positioning, dual aspect light, panoramic golf views, and a large entertainment-led floor plate. Most suited to families that host frequently.

slides.title: The Wraparound Residence
slides.subtitle: Corner exposure. Two aspects. One quiet horizon.
slides.body: A corner positioning grants the residence two open elevations — golf course to the south, landscaped sanctuary to the east. Mornings arrive softly through the foyer; evenings settle across the living wing.

images.alt: Twin towers of The Camellias at golden hour

slides.title: An open arrival, a private wing.
slides.body: Public spaces are carried along the south face — living, dining, lounge. The private wing folds inward, layered behind a soft transition foyer. Bedrooms each open to their own balcony or bay.

images.alt: Architectural envelope of a Type A residence

slides.title: Configuration & Areas
slides.subtitle: Indicative — to be confirmed with the sales team.

slides.title: Light, gathered along the long edge.
slides.body: A 12-metre living edge runs uninterrupted along the south face. Sliding panels retract fully into wall recesses, dissolving the threshold between living room and wrap balcony.

images.alt: Pool deck with golden hour reflections

slides.title: Four bedrooms. Four moods.
slides.body: Each bedroom is paired with its own dressing line, ensuite and outdoor extension. The master commands the corner — a quiet pivot point between the two open aspects.
